text.title: Cloud Run Autoscaling Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Cloud Run Autoscaling` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Per-instance concurrency MUST be tuned (not default 80) using load tests + p95 latency target; documented in service README with measurement date.
rule.rationale: Default 80 is rarely optimal; tuning typically halves cost or doubles throughput.

rule.statement: Latency-sensitive endpoints (p99 < 500ms target) MUST set min-instances ≥ 1 to avoid cold starts; min-instances cost MUST be documented in spec.
rule.rationale: Cold starts cost 500ms-2s on Cloud Run; min-instances kills that for latency-critical paths.

rule.statement: Max-instances MUST be capped at sustainable downstream capacity (e.g. DB connection pool, third-party rate limit); default unlimited is forbidden in prod.

rationale.source: Cloud Run autoscaling docs; downstream-overload postmortems

rule.rationale: Unlimited scaling overloads the next hop (DB, vendor API); explicit cap protects downstream.

rule.statement: `cpu-always-allocated = true` MUST be justified in spec (background work, websockets); default request-based allocation otherwise.
rule.rationale: Always-allocated CPU doubles cost; only justified when background work occurs between requests.
Without metrics, autoscaling tuning is guesswork; dashboard + alarms keep the system honest.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
